# Basic SQLite server test with aggressive timeouts

# Set aggressive timeouts for rapid testing
env MCP_TEST_TIMEOUT=2s
env MCP_SERVER_TIMEOUT=1s
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=1s
env SQLITE_DB_PATH=$WORK/test.db

# Start the server with aggressive timeout
exec timeout 10s go run . &
wait 200ms

# Test get_schema on empty database
cat > schema_request.json << EOF
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "get_schema",
    "arguments": {}
  }
}
EOF

bash 'timeout 2s echo "$(<schema_request.json)" | mcpcat -'
stdout '\[\]|empty'

# Test creating a table with aggressive timeout
cat > create_table.json << EOF
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0", 
  "id": 2,
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "execute_statement",
    "arguments": {
      "statement": "CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, email TEXT)"
    }
  }
}
EOF

bash 'timeout 2s echo "$(<create_table.json)" | mcpcat -'
stdout 'executed successfully|Rows affected'

# Test inserting data with tight timeout
cat > insert_data.json << EOF
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 3, 
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "execute_statement",
    "arguments": {
      "statement": "INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('John Doe', 'john@example.com')"
    }
  }
}
EOF

bash 'timeout 2s echo "$(<insert_data.json)" | mcpcat -'
stdout 'executed successfully|Rows affected: 1'

# Test querying data with rapid response requirement
cat > query_data.json << EOF
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 4,
  "method": "tools/call", 
  "params": {
    "name": "execute_query",
    "arguments": {
      "query": "SELECT * FROM users"
    }
  }
}
EOF

bash 'timeout 2s echo "$(<query_data.json)" | mcpcat -'
stdout '"name": "John Doe"'
stdout '"email": "john@example.com"'

# Test schema retrieval after table creation
bash 'timeout 2s echo "$(<schema_request.json)" | mcpcat -'
stdout '"name": "users"'
stdout '"type": "table"'

# Cleanup
exec rm -f schema_request.json create_table.json insert_data.json query_data.json
